title: Preferences Object
description: The Preferences object provides access to the various preference related objects for getting and setting the various preference values.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: apiPreferences
properties.description: Gets the APIPreferences object, which provides access to the various preferences associated with the API.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

apiPreferences.title: Preferences.apiPreferences Property
apiPreferences.description: Gets the APIPreferences object, which provides access to the various preferences associated with the API.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.apiPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.defaultUnitsPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.generalPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.graphicsPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.gridPreferences

isValid.title: Preferences.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.isValid

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.materialPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.networkPreferences

objectType.title: Preferences.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.objectType

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.productPreferences

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.productUsageData

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = preferences_var.unitAndValuePreferences

classType.title: Preferences.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
